latest.title: New Website
latest.content:
1 Jul 2026 08:00
RNS Number : 5642K
dotDigital Group plc
01 July 2026
1 July 2026
Dotdigital Group plc
("Dotdigital" or the "Group")
New Website
Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD), the leading SaaS provider of an AI-powered customer experience and data platform for intelligent personalised marketing engagement at scale, announces the launch of its new website alongside a refresh of the Dotdigital commercial brand.
The new corporate website is accessible at
https://dotdigital.com/
. Investor relations and AIM Rule 26 information will now be accessible at
investors.dotdigital.com
.
The website launch coincides with the Group's commercial rebrand and reflects Dotdigital's evolution into an all-in-one customer experience and data platform, supported by sustained investment in AI, data and personalisation and the acquisitions of Fresh Relevance, Social Snowball and Alia Software. Following a global, digital launch today, the new branding and product roadmap will be presented at
Dotdigital Summit
on 2 July, the Group's flagship customer event.
Milan Patel, CEO of Dotdigital, commented:
"
Our new brand and website are a clear expression of our market positioning and the value we deliver to more than 9,800 customers globally. We remain focused on maintaining the pace of product innovation, with further enhancements to come as we continue to expand the capability of the platform through our AI-focused roadmap."
Contacts
Dotdigital Group plc
+44 (0)20 3953 3072
Milan Patel, CEO
investorrelations@dotdigital.com
Tom Mullan, CFO
Alma Strategic Communications
+44 (0)20 3405 0210
Hilary Buchanan
dotdigital@almastrategic.com
David Ison
Will Merison
Canaccord Genuity (Nominated Adviser and Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Cavendish Capital Markets Limited (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7220 0500
Jonny Franklin-Adams, Corporate Finance
Sunila de Silva, Equity Capital Markets
Singer Capital Markets (Joint Broker)
+44 (0)20 7496 3000
Shaun Dobson
Jen Boorer
About Dotdigital
Dotdigital Group plc (AIM: DOTD) is a leading provider of customer experience and data platform technology to marketing professionals. Dotdigital's CXDP combines the power of automation, data and AI to help businesses deliver hyper-relevant customer experiences at scale. With Dotdigital, marketing teams can unify and enrich their customer data, identify valuable customer segments, and deliver personalised cross-channel customer journeys that result in engagements, conversions, and loyalty.
Founded in 1999, Dotdigital is headquartered in London with offices in Manchester, Southampton, New York, Melbourne, Sydney, Singapore, Tokyo, Warsaw and Cape Town. Dotdigital's solutions empower over 9,800 brands across 150 countries.

company_30s.what_they_do: Dot Digital Ltd. sells cloud‑based marketing automation software that lets brands send email, SMS, push and social messages, build customer journeys and analyse campaign performance.
company_30s.why_they_matter: In a world where brands are chasing personalized customer experiences, Dot Digital offers a single platform that integrates data, creative, and analytics. Its solutions help marketers shift from generic blasts to targeted, measurable campaigns, giving it a foothold in the fast‑growing marketing‑tech market.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has been restructuring its leadership and recently announced the acquisition of Alia, a niche data‑integration partner, to broaden its product suite. Cash flow has been tight, but the company is actively working to improve profitability through cost cuts and the new CRO’s sales initiatives. The share is trading near its lowest point of the year, well below the valuation of comparable peers.

rns_narrative: Dot Digital’s board has taken several steps to realign the company: a new chief revenue officer was appointed, directors disclosed their shareholdings, and the firm has begun buying back its own shares, signalling confidence in the underlying business.

Strategically, the acquisition of Alia is a move to plug data gaps and strengthen the platform’s analytics engine, potentially unlocking new revenue streams. The interim results show a narrowing loss, while the half‑year update hints at a turnaround plan that hinges on the integration of Alia’s technology.
Overall, the company is moving from a defensive cash‑burn phase to an active growth push, with leadership changes and a fresh acquisition aimed at stabilising earnings and positioning for a rebound.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of traders voicing optimism about the Alia deal.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Traders fear that AI‑driven competitors could erode Dot Digital’s market share, and the company’s recent losses and leadership churn have raised doubts about its ability to deliver the promised turnaround.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from neutral to mildly bearish; the few bullish posts about the acquisition are drowned out by concerns over AI disruption and the company’s historical volatility.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – no public interest has built up, which means a surprise catalyst could generate a sharp spike in price.

near_term_catalysts: Q2 earnings release – a positive surprise on revenue or margin could validate the turnaround plan.
Integration milestones from the Alia acquisition – any announcement of a new joint product or revenue share would lift sentiment.
The CRO’s first quarterly sales figures – a jump in pipeline or win rate would confirm the new sales strategy.
A share‑repurchase announcement or dividend declaration would signal confidence in the share price.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that breaks the 20‑day average while the price approaches the 50‑day moving average. A clear bounce off the 2026 March low would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis. Any announcement that the Alia integration is ahead of schedule or that the CRO has secured new large accounts would be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of capitulation – the price has fallen sharply, volume has thinned, and there is no active discussion. If the next catalyst arrives before the end of Q2, we can expect a swift bounce; if it stalls, the recovery will likely be slower.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The deep crash (over 70 % from peak) creates a classic asymmetry: a small downside risk for a potential upside that could exceed 200 % if the price recovers to the mid‑52‑week range. The recent acquisition and leadership changes add a catalyst that can kick the mean reversion into motion. Historically, deep‑bottom signals have delivered the highest pay‑offs, and the current setup matches that profile.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to deliver on the Alia integration or the CRO cannot grow the pipeline, the price could stay stuck at the low. A further loss would be limited by the already deep trough, but the upside would be capped. The worst‑case scenario is a continued decline to a new low, which would require a significant new catalyst to reverse.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 10–15 % of the entry price if the bounce stalls. Potential gain: 200–300 % if the stock rebounds to the mid‑year high. Risk‑reward ratio: roughly 1:20, a textbook contrarian asymmetry.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 2026 March low (approx. £0.02) with a 1‑to‑2 risk‑reward structure. Allocate 10 % of discretionary capital, set a stop 10 % below the entry, and trail it to lock in gains as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until volume spikes to 2‑3× the 20‑day average while the price hits the 50‑day moving average. If Q2 results beat revenue expectations, add a larger stake.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks. The only real danger would be a fundamental collapse of the marketing‑tech market, which is unlikely given the sector’s growth.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Dot Digital is sitting on a deep, 70 %+ crash with a clear catalyst in the Alia acquisition; if the Q2 earnings surprise or CRO pipeline growth materialises, the upside is 200 %+, making it a compelling contrarian play.
